brief_justification: Paper A is a hardware-performance engineering paper: it implements molecular-dynamics force loops on an NVIDIA GPU in CUDA and reports empirical speedup factors versus a CPU. Paper B is an exactly-solvable-probability paper: it derives the free-energy distribution of the continuum directed polymer / KPZ equation via a rigorous steepest-descent analysis of the Tracy-Widom formula for ASEP in a weakly-asymmetric scaling limit. There is no role-for-role correspondence between GPU parallelization/benchmarking and integrable-probability asymptotics; the only overlap is the loose 'physics simulation' theme, i.e. shared vocabulary, not shared machinery.
